skill: One-variable data: Distributions and measures of center and spread
stem:
Data set P: 81, 82, 83, 86, 88

Data set Q: 75, 78, 90, 92, x

Data set P and data set Q each contain 5 numbers. If the mean of data set P is equal to the mean of data set Q, what is the value of x?
rationale:
Choice B is correct. The mean of data set P is (81+82+83+86+88) ÷ 5 = 420 ÷ 5 = 84. For the mean of data set Q to equal 84, the sum of its values must equal 420. Thus 75 + 78 + 90 + 92 + x = 420, so x = 420 − 335 = 85.

Choice A is incorrect. This results from a computational error.

Choice C is incorrect. This is one of the values already in data set Q, not the value of x.

Choice D is incorrect. This results from a computational error.
